req.title: Recurring transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions by tier (standard/premium), four boundary points ($1, $2500, $2501, $10000, $10001), an unsupported-frequency error path, and a state transition from 'scheduled' to 'next_run calculated'. I selected: (1) EP positive – premium tier mid-range amount; (2) NEG – standard tier amount exceeding $2500 cap; (3) BVA – premium tier at exact $10000 maximum; (4) BVA – standard tier at exact $2500 maximum; (5) NEG – unsupported frequency value; (6) DT – standard tier at $2501, crossing the boundary. Together these give positive, negative, and boundary coverage mandated by the task, while exercising all cited heuristics.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit
actions: Authenticate as a premium-tier customer via <fixture: auth_token_premium>
expected: Response body contains 'next_run' with a valid ISO-8601 date
trace: R-FIN-030: premium tier allowed up to $10,000 per occurrence
R-FIN-030: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit(api_client, auth_token_premium, next_valid_date):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_premium}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "start_date": next_valid_date,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run" in body
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    <assert_iso8601>(body["next_run"])

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as a standard-tier customer via <fixture: auth_token_standard>
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=3000 (exceeds $2500 standard cap), frequency='weekly'
expected: Response body contains error code indicating per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422(api_client, auth_token_standard, next_valid_date):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_standard}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 3000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "weekly",
        "start_date": next_valid_date,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" not in body
    assert <error_code_matches>(body, "LIMIT_EXCEEDED")

tests.name: test_recurring_transfer_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as a premium-tier customer via <fixture: auth_token_premium>
expected: Response body contains error indicating unsupported frequency
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_recurring_transfer_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(api_client, auth_token_premium, next_valid_date):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_premium}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "hourly",
        "start_date": next_valid_date,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" not in body
    assert <error_code_matches>(body, "UNSUPPORTED_FREQUENCY")

tests.name: test_standard_tier_one_cent_above_cap_rejected
actions: Authenticate as a standard-tier customer via <fixture: auth_token_standard>
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2501 ($1 above standard cap), frequency='monthly'
expected: Response body contains per-occurrence limit error
trace: R-FIN-030: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500 (off-point above cap rejected)
DT: standard tier x amount>2500 -> reject
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_tier_one_cent_above_cap_rejected(api_client, auth_token_standard, next_valid_date):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_standard}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "start_date": next_valid_date,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" not in body
    assert <error_code_matches>(body, "LIMIT_EXCEEDED")
